sources.llm_title: Master of Information Management and Systems | UC Berkeley School of Information
sources.llm_summary: Program overview describing MIMS as a two-year STEM-designated, full-time professional master’s program for technology leaders and data-driven decision-makers, with interdisciplinary coverage across technology, people, and policy.

sources.llm_title: Information Management & Systems MIMS | Berkeley Graduate Division
sources.llm_summary: Graduate Division program page describing MIMS as a two-year full-time program for information professionals and outlining the program’s broad learning goals.

sources.llm_title: For New MIMS Students | UC Berkeley School of Information
sources.llm_summary: Student-facing page noting that School of Information courses bridge information and computer science, design, social sciences, management, law, and policy.

llm_rationales.note: Selected interdisciplinary, professional, STEM-adjacent, and digital because the program is described as a 'uniquely interdisciplinary curriculum that bridges technology, people, and policy' and as a 'two-year STEM-designated, full-time professional master’s program' for technology leaders and data-driven decision-makers.

Supported digital and professional orientation, plus interdisciplinary breadth, by listing focus areas such as human-computer interaction, user experience design, applied data science and machine learning, information policy and ethical technology, and entrepreneurship.

Did not add ui_ux or human_comptuer_interaction because the evidence mentions UX and HCI as focus areas, but the strongest overall program-level positioning is broader than a dedicated UI/UX or HCI specialization.
The program page describes MIMS as a 'two-year' full-time professional master's program; at Berkeley, two years corresponds to four standard semesters.
Berkeley Graduate Division describes the MIMS program as a 'two-year full-time program,' supporting the four-semester classification.

Used the School of Information tuition page snippet stating estimated total tuition and fees for a new student starting Fall 2025 are $82,096. This is tuition + fees only, suitable for cross-program comparison.

Used the same estimated total tuition and fees figure of $82,096 in USD as the program-stated total degree cost in base currency. The evidence supports a single tuition-and-fees total, not a full cost of attendance.

Did not use the funding page's Total Expected Graduate Costs because that is a bundled cost-of-attendance figure including personal expenses; per rules, bundled COA totals are not appropriate for comparison_cost_usd without an isolated tuition-and-fees subtotal.
The fetched MIMS degree-requirements material does not name any required courses, course codes, or official titles, so core_courses is left empty.

identity.program_name: Master of Information Management and Systems (MIMS)
identity.credential_name: Master of Information Management and Systems

positioning.positioning_summary: MIMS is positioned as a professional, interdisciplinary program for information professionals and technology leaders, bridging technology, people, policy, and related fields. Its examples and focus areas emphasize human-computer interaction, user experience, applied data science, information policy, and entrepreneurship, with a clear orientation toward digital and STEM-adjacent work.

curriculum.curriculum_summary: MIMS is a two-year, full-time, STEM-designated professional master’s program. The fetched materials do not provide a detailed course-by-course requirement list, but they do indicate that the School of Information’s coursework spans information and computer science, design, social sciences, management, law, and policy, and that the degree includes a formal final project component.
curriculum.elective_requirements: No specific required electives, elective counts, or elective areas are named in the fetched degree-requirements material.
